approachDetail: HTTP-direct via RoboBrowser (a thin wrapper over the `requests` library + BeautifulSoup). NO real browser, NO JS execution. RoboBrowser is constructed with `RoboBrowser(parser="html5lib")`. It maintains a `requests.Session` (`browser.session`) whose cookie jar is read/written directly. Forms are discovered via BeautifulSoup parsing and submitted as standard HTTP POSTs. So while the README/library name says "browser", it is fundamentally screen-scraping over requests, exactly the HTTP-direct reference described.
repo: https://github.com/schwalle/kicktipp-betbot (git remote origin; local clone is single visible commit 37f3385 "Merge pull request #11 from schwalle/development")
language: Python 3 (CI pins Python 3.8). Dependencies (requirements.txt, exact pins): docopt==0.6.2 (CLI arg parsing from docstring), robobro==0.5.3 (this is the PyPI package name that provides the `robobrowser` module — RoboBrowser, built on requests + BeautifulSoup), html5lib==1.1 (HTML parser passed to RoboBrowser). Predictors use only stdlib `math`. Tests use pytest. No requests/bs4 pinned directly — pulled transitively by robobro.

auth.loginUrl: http://www.kicktipp.de/info/profil/login (built as URL_BASE + '/info/profil/login'; URL_BASE = 'http://www.kicktipp.de' — plain HTTP, no TLS, no www->https redirect handling beyond what requests does automatically)
auth.csrf: NONE. No CSRF token is read, stored, or sent anywhere in the code. perform_login() just opens the login page, fills the FIRST form returned by browser.get_form() (no form id/name/selector specified), sets form['kennung'] and form['passwort'], and submits. Any hidden token fields present on the page would be carried verbatim by RoboBrowser's form serialization, but the code is unaware of them. Bet submission likewise relies only on the 'login' cookie + whatever hidden fields RoboBrowser auto-includes from the parsed tippabgabe form.
auth.sessionReuse: Token = the value of the 'login' cookie after a successful login, captured via browser.session.cookies['login'] (returned by login()). With --get-login-token the tool logs in interactively then prints this cookie value to stdout for the user to save. On later runs, --use-login-token <token> skips interactive login entirely: it injects the saved value back with browser.session.cookies['login'] = token, then performs all subsequent requests authenticated purely by that single cookie. There is NO file/keyring persistence inside the tool — the user is responsible for storing the token string (intended for cron usage). Note: even when logging in interactively (no token arg), main() still re-sets browser.session.cookies['login'] = token after login.
auth.notes: Login-success detection is heuristic and fragile: logged_in() does browser.find('div', content="Login") and treats presence of a div whose content equals 'Login' as 'still on login page' => not logged in. get_credentials() prompts interactively: input('Username: ') and getpass.getpass(prompt='Password: '). Loops until login succeeds. No rate-limiting, no retry/backoff, no 2FA handling, no handling of cookie expiry (a stale token just silently fails downstream parsing). Plain HTTP means credentials/cookie can traverse unencrypted unless the server upgrades the connection.

dataFormats: "INPUT: CLI flags only (parsed by docopt from the module docstring). Credentials via interactive stdin prompts (input() + getpass). The login token is passed as a raw CLI string argument (--use-login-token). No config file, no env vars, no JSON/YAML config. Communities passed as positional CLI args. OUTPUT: human-readable plaintext to stdout only — one line per match like \"02.10.2020 20:30 'Home' vs. 'Away'  (2.15;3.5;3.3) - betting 1:2\", plus status lines (skipped / not betting yet / no bets possible / Dry run). --get-login-token prints just the bare cookie value. No structured (JSON/CSV) output, no machine-readable mode, no logging framework, no exit-code semantics beyond exit()/sys.exit() on errors."
oddsLogic: "YES — predicts a scoreline purely from the 1X2 betting odds scraped from row[4] (home rate, draw/deuce rate, road rate; only home & road rates are used). Match exposes rate_home, rate_deuce, rate_road, odds tuple. TWO predictors: (1) SimplePredictor: diff = |rate_home - rate_road|; if diff < DRAW_THRESHOLD(1.2) -> (1,1) draw; else bucket by DOMINATION_THRESHOLD(6): diff>=6 -> (3,1); diff>=3 -> (2,1); else (1,0). home_wins = (rate_home < rate_road) i.e. lower odds = favorite; if not home_wins the tuple is reversed. (2) CalculationPredictor (continuous): constants MAX_GOALS=5, DOMINATION_THRESHOLD=9, DRAW_THRESHOLD=1.3, NONLINEARITY=0.5. difference=|rate_home-rate_road|; if difference<1.3 -> (1,1). totalGoals = round(min(difference/9, 1) * 5). ratio = ((max(rate_home,rate_road)/min(...)) / (rate_home+rate_road)) ** 0.5. winner=round(totalGoals*ratio); looser=round(totalGoals*(1-ratio)); if winner<=looser: winner+=1. Lower odds (favorite) gets 'winner' goals: if rate_home>rate_road (home is underdog) return (looser,winner) else (winner,looser). Note both use ONLY the bookmaker odds already shown on kicktipp's tippabgabe page (the 'Quote' column) — no external odds API, no team strength model."
strengths: Pure HTTP/requests via RoboBrowser — fast, headless, no browser binary, cron/Raspberry-Pi friendly (the stated design goal)
Clean token-reuse model: login once, capture the single 'login' cookie, replay it forever with --use-login-token — avoids storing raw credentials and avoids re-login per run
Pluggable predictor architecture: PredictorBase + auto-discovery via pkgutil.iter_modules + importlib + PredictorBase.__subclasses__(); drop a .py in predictors/ and it's selectable by class name
Sensible safety defaults: does NOT override existing bets unless --override-bets; --dry-run to preview; --deadline to bet only near kickoff
Multi-community support in one run; defaults to all communities
spieltagIndex support to pin a specific matchday (good real-world fix for postponed-match navigation quirk)
Has unit tests (URL building, predictors) and a GitHub Actions CI that lints + tests + runs --help
docopt gives a single source of truth for usage/flags
weaknesses: Built on robobrowser/robobro 0.5.3 — long-unmaintained, incompatible with modern Werkzeug/Python; the dependency stack is effectively rotted (~2021).
Plain HTTP base URL ('http://www.kicktipp.de') — credentials and the reusable 'login' cookie can be sent unencrypted; should be https.

Extremely brittle scraping by POSITIONAL td index (row[0..4]) and 'first tbody' — any layout change to kicktipp's tippabgabe table breaks score/odds/date extraction silently or with a bare except that does sys.exit('Error: Not enough data...').
Login-success check is a fragile heuristic (find a div whose content == 'Login'); easily wrong after any markup/i18n change.
No CSRF/token awareness at all; relies entirely on RoboBrowser silently re-submitting hidden form fields — fragile if kicktipp adds explicit token validation.
Bare `except:` around match construction masks real errors and force-exits the whole program on a single bad row.

is_before_dealine uses now=dt.datetime.now() as a DEFAULT ARG — evaluated once at import time, so 'now' is frozen for the process lifetime (classic Python mutable/default-arg-at-import bug); also compares to naive local datetimes with no timezone handling.
Hardcoded matchday range 1..34 (Bundesliga-specific); breaks for other competitions/league sizes.
No structured output (no JSON), no logging, no real error/exit-code contract — hard to automate/monitor beyond cron-and-pray.
No persistence/refresh of the token; expired cookie just yields confusing downstream scrape failures.
No tests for auth, scraping, or submission — only URL building and predictor instantiation are covered.
Predictors only consume the odds already on the page; no external data, no calibration, no per-league tuning.

approachDetail: Pure Selenium (Chromium + chromedriver) browser automation. Logs in via the real login form, navigates to the competition's tippabgabe page, scrapes the games table and betting odds from the rendered DOM, computes scores from the odds, types them into the per-game input fields, and clicks submit. No direct HTTP/API calls to KickTipp at all (only the browser drives KickTipp); plain `requests` is used solely for outbound notification webhooks (Zapier/ntfy/generic/heartbeat). Runs as a long-lived daemon with an internal while-True scheduler and an HTTP health server.
language: Python 3.10 (Docker base python:3.10.13-alpine). Libraries: selenium>=4.21.0, requests>=2.25.1, sentry_sdk>=2.35.0. Stdlib http.server for health endpoint, zoneinfo for TZ handling. Package layout: src/kicktipp_bot with core/, models/, utils/, webdriver/ submodules; entrypoint run.py.

However CHANGELOG.md shows a healthy, regularly-released upstream: latest 3.3.0 (2026-03-30), prior 3.2.2 (2025-12-08), 3.2.1 (2025-11-17), 3.2.0 (2025-08-25), 3.1.0 (2025-08-20), etc. Uses git-cliff (cliff.toml) for conventional-commit changelog generation, dependabot, issue/PR templates, three GitHub Actions workflows (main push->GHCR build + k8s rollout restart, PR checks, release). Conclusion: actively maintained upstream, well-engineered, multi-release cadence over ~1 year.

auth.csrf: none — no CSRF token handling anywhere in code. Login is done purely through Selenium by filling the real form and clicking submit, so any KickTipp-issued session/CSRF cookies are managed implicitly by the browser session and never inspected.
auth.sessionReuse: NONE. Session lives only inside the live Selenium Chrome process. driver.quit() is called in _cleanup() after every single run (run_bot() creates a fresh WebDriver, logs in, tips, then quits). So the bot logs in from scratch on EVERY scheduled cycle. No cookie jar, no persisted profile dir, no token caching. Credentials come from env vars KICKTIPP_EMAIL / KICKTIPP_PASSWORD.
auth.notes: Login verification (_verify_login) is brittle: it asserts driver.current_url == Config.BASE_URL ('https://www.kicktipp.de/') exactly; any other landing URL is treated as failure. Email field located By.ID 'kennung', password By.ID 'passwort', submit By.NAME 'submitbutton'. A separate cookie-consent acceptor (accept_terms_and_conditions) clicks XPath //*[@id="qc-cmp2-ui"]/div[2]/div/button[2] (Quantcast CMP) but this method is defined and NOT called from the login flow; the tipping page has its own SourcePoint iframe consent handler instead.

oddsLogic: YES — calculate_tip() in models/game.py. Inputs: home win odds = quotes[0] (label '1'), away win odds = quotes[2] (label '2'); the draw odds quotes[1] ('X') is parsed/validated but NOT used in the calc. Algorithm: quote_difference = home_quote - away_quote (negative => home favored). random_goal = random.randint(0,1) (a 0-or-1 jitter reused for the loser's score and added to winner). coefficient = 0.3 if abs(quote_difference) > 7 else 0.75. Then: (1) if abs(quote_difference) < 0.25 => predict a draw: return (random_goal, random_goal) i.e. 0-0 or 1-1. (2) if quote_difference < 0 (home favored): home_goals = max(0, round(-quote_difference * coefficient)) + random_goal; away_goals = random_goal. (3) else (away favored): home_goals = random_goal; away_goals = max(0, round(quote_difference * coefficient)) + random_goal. Net effect: bigger odds gap => bigger predicted winning margin, dampened to 0.3 for blowouts (gap>7) so scores don't explode, plus +0/+1 randomness so repeated runs vary. Quotes are validated by Game._validate_quotes(): exactly 3 entries, each float()-parseable, else ValueError. NOTE: assumes German decimal odds already use '.'; no comma->dot normalization is done.
features: Auto-login to KickTipp with env-var credentials
Auto-tip all upcoming games in one competition by scraping bookmaker odds and computing a score
Skips games already started (game_time <= now Europe/Berlin)
Time-window gating: only tips games starting within KICKTIPP_HOURS_UNTIL_GAME (default 2h)
Skips already-tipped games unless OVERWRITE_TIPS=true
Cookie/consent dialog auto-accept (SourcePoint iframe 'Akzeptieren')
Internal scheduler: re-runs every KICKTIPP_RUN_EVERY_X_MINUTES (default 60; 0 = run once then exit)
Notifications: Zapier webhook, ntfy (basic auth), generic JSON webhook (e.g. Home Assistant)
GROUP_NOTIFICATIONS mode: batch all tips of a run into a single webhook call at the end
HTTP health server with /health (200/503) and /status (detailed JSON stats) on port 8080

dataFormats: Input: environment variables only (no config file, no CLI args beyond --debug/--headless). Required: KICKTIPP_EMAIL, KICKTIPP_PASSWORD, KICKTIPP_NAME_OF_COMPETITION. Optional: KICKTIPP_RUN_EVERY_X_MINUTES (default 60), KICKTIPP_HOURS_UNTIL_GAME (default 2), OVERWRITE_TIPS (default false), CHROMEDRIVER_PATH, ZAPIER_URL, NTFY_URL/NTFY_USERNAME/NTFY_PASSWORD, WEBHOOK_URL, GROUP_NOTIFICATIONS, HEARTBEAT_URL, HEALTH_CHECK_PORT (default 8080), SENTRY_DSN/SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT, TZ, PYTHONUNBUFFERED. Output: (1) stdout logs (logging, format '%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s'); (2) writes tips into the live KickTipp form; (3) JSON over HTTP at /health and /status; (4) outbound webhooks. Zapier payload (form-encoded for single, JSON for grouped) fields: date(ISO), team1, team2, quoteteam1, quotedraw, quoteteam2, tipteam1, tipteam2. Generic webhook JSON: home_team, away_team, quotes[], tip[], time('%d.%m.%y %H:%M'), timestamp(ISO). ntfy: X-Title header + plain text body. NotificationEvent.to_dict() merges both generic and Zapier field sets.
strengths: Very defensive scraping layer (SeleniumUtils) with retries, timeouts, stale-element re-finding, and safe_* wrappers around every find/click/text/attr call
Robust multi-format odds parser: handles ad-anchor, ad-free-span, and legacy quote-link DOMs, with label-based mapping {'1','X','2'} rather than positional assumptions
Timezone-correct: all times parsed/compared in Europe/Berlin via zoneinfo (avoids 'game already started' bugs across server TZ)
Clean modular architecture (config / webdriver / auth / game_tipper / table_processors / notifications / models / health) — easy to read and adapt
Time-state machine for rowheader-vs-datarow handles KickTipp's pattern where kickoff time appears once in a header and subsequent rows inherit it (td[1] hidden via 'hide' class)
Production-grade deployment story: Dockerfile with HEALTHCHECK, docker-compose, full k8s manifests with startup/liveness/readiness probes + resource limits, GHCR build + auto rollout-restart on push
Good observability: structured health stats (success rate, run counts, uptime), Sentry, optional heartbeat pings
Safety guards: skip-started, time-window gating, don't-overwrite-existing-tips toggle
weaknesses: Entire approach hinges on Selenium + headless Chromium — heavy (alpine+chromium image, 400-800Mi memory in k8s), slow, and fragile vs DOM/CSS changes; no direct HTTP/form-POST path
Re-logs-in from scratch every cycle (driver.quit() each run); no session/cookie persistence => unnecessary auth load and slower runs

Scheduling is a naive in-process while-True + sleep loop (not real cron/k8s CronJob); requires the pod to run 24/7 just to fire periodically, and the health server exists largely to keep that long-lived pod alive

calculate_tip() is simplistic and non-deterministic: ignores draw odds entirely, injects random.randint(0,1) so scores aren't reproducible, no implied-probability/Poisson modeling, magic constants (0.25, 0.3, 0.75, gap>7)
Assumes odds already in dot-decimal; no German comma normalization, no handling of missing/half odds; if any of the 3 odds missing it just skips the game
Single competition per instance (one slug from env); no multi-competition / multi-account support without running N containers
Login success check is exact-URL equality to base — likely to false-negative on redirects or locale variants
send_default_pii=True in Sentry can leak user data; credentials only via plain env vars
No CLI subcommands, no dry-run that prints without submitting (debug just pauses), no JSON/structured output mode, not an MCP server
No tests of the tip logic in the repo (tests.yaml only builds the Docker image); no unit coverage of selectors or calculate_tip
Brittle XPath like //*[@id="qc-cmp2-ui"]/div[2]/div/button[2] for consent (and that one is dead code)

DO BETTER — robust auth verification (check for a logged-in marker element/account menu, not exact base-URL equality), German comma->dot odds normalization, and graceful handling of partial/missing odds; add unit tests for calculate_tip and selector parsing fixtures (upstream has none).
SECURITY — do not set Sentry send_default_pii=True by default and don't pass chromedriver path positionally (deprecated in Selenium 4); keep secrets out of logs.
isMcpOrCli: Neither an MCP server nor a real CLI tool — it is a long-running daemon/script. Entry: run.py -> kicktipp_bot.main:main(). It accepts only two bare flags parsed by scanning sys.argv: '--debug' (DEBUG logging + 20s review pause) and '--headless' (adds Chrome headless flags). No argparse, no subcommands, no MCP protocol/tools. Architecture: KicktippBot.run() per-cycle => WebDriverManager.create_driver() (Chrome; honors CHROMEDRIVER_PATH or --headless) -> Authenticator.login() (fill kennung/passwort, click submitbutton, verify url==base) -> GameTipper.tip_all_games() (navigate to {slug}/tippabgabe, accept SourcePoint consent, count datarows in #tippabgabeSpiele, walk rows via TableRowProcessor maintaining rowheader time-state, per datarow extract teams td[2]/td[3], get heimTipp/gastTipp inputs, extract odds via GameDataExtractor, Game.calculate_tip(), type scores, then click submitbutton) -> NotificationManager fan-out -> driver.quit(). main() wraps this in while True with sleep(RUN_EVERY_X_MINUTES) (0 => single run), runs a stdlib HTTP health server thread (/health, /status on :8080), and optionally inits Sentry and posts to HEARTBEAT_URL. Deployment: Docker (python:3.10.13-alpine + chromium/chromium-chromedriver), docker-compose, and k8s Deployment (1 replica, RollingUpdate, probes, 250m-500m CPU / 400-800Mi mem); CI builds amd64 image to GHCR and does 'kubectl rollout restart' on push to main.

approachDetail: NOT selenium — uses Playwright (headless Chromium). I selected the closest enum but the actual approach is: Playwright drives a headless Chromium browser to navigate kicktipp.com, then `cheerio.load(await page.content())` parses the rendered HTML. For WRITE operations (placing bets) it uses Playwright's native DOM interaction: `page.$(selector)` + `element.fill()` for score inputs, `page.selectOption()` for bonus dropdowns, and `page.click('button[name="submitbutton"]')` wrapped in `Promise.all([page.waitForNavigation(), ...])`. For READ operations it goes through Playwright navigation then hands the static HTML to Cheerio. There is NO direct HTTP/fetch — every request is a full browser page load (~2-5s startup each). NOTE: the enum lacked a 'playwright' option matching, but the repo explicitly uses the `playwright` npm package v1.40.0, chromium.launch({headless:true}).
language: TypeScript (ES2022 target, Node16 module resolution, ESM with .js import extensions). Key deps: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.27.1, playwright ^1.40.0 (headless Chromium), cheerio ^1.0.0 (HTML parsing), commander ^13.0.0 (CLI), ini ^5.0.0 (config file), ora ^8.0.0 (spinner), zod (via MCP SDK, for tool param schemas). Dev: typescript ^5.7.0, vitest ^3.0.0, @types/node ^22.
maintenance: SINGLE COMMIT repo. `git log -1` = 2026-03-07 21:20:49 +0100, commit count = 1, first commit = same date. Only branch is main. Effectively a one-shot publish / snapshot — no iterative history, no maintenance trail, no issues/PR activity visible from git. Forked/rewritten from schwalle/kicktipp-betbot (Python original). Appears to be a freshly-published, possibly abandoned-after-launch project. Low maintenance signal: zero follow-up commits.

auth.csrf: none — no CSRF token handling found anywhere in the code. Login just fills kennung+passwort and clicks button[type="submit"]. Bet submission just clicks button[name="submitbutton"] with the form's existing hidden fields (whatever the page rendered). No token is read, stored, or replayed.
auth.sessionReuse: Playwright storageState persisted to ~/.config/kicktipp-agent/session.json (chmod 600). On launchBrowser(): if session.json exists, create context with storageState:session.json, goto URL_BASE, and if the resulting url does NOT include '/login' the session is considered valid and reused (skips login entirely). If redirected to /login, it closes the context and does a fresh login, then re-saves storageState. The MCP server ALSO keeps a single in-process persistent Browser+Page+Context alive across all tool calls (module-level browserInstance/pageInstance/contextInstance); getPage() probes liveness with page.evaluate(()=>true) and re-launches if dead. Cookie names are NOT hardcoded — whatever kicktipp sets (typically 'login' long-lived cookie + JSESSIONID) is captured wholesale by Playwright storageState.
auth.notes: Credentials precedence: (1) env vars KICKTIPP_EMAIL + KICKTIPP_PASSWORD, else (2) ~/.config/kicktipp-agent/config.ini [auth] section. CLI prompts interactively (with manual raw-mode password masking) on first run if neither present; MCP server CANNOT prompt and errors out telling user to set env vars or run `kicktipp set-community`. Passwords in config.ini are encrypted with AES-256-GCM but the key is DERIVED DETERMINISTICALLY from sha256('kicktipp-agent:'+os.hostname()+':'+os.userInfo().username) — i.e. NOT a real secret, anyone on the same machine/user can decrypt. Stored as 'enc.'+base64(iv[16]+authTag[16]+ciphertext). Plaintext passwords are auto-migrated to 'enc.' on read. Login failure detection = url still contains '/login' after submit → process.exit(1) (CLI) with message 'Login failed. Check your credentials (use --logout to re-enter).' Note the message says --logout but it's actually the `logout` subcommand. Cookie consent CMP handled by dismissConsent(): waits for iframe[src*="privacy-mgmt"], clicks button:has-text("Accept and continue") inside any frame, waits for iframe hidden; 2s/3s timeouts, swallows errors.

Fetch + submit bonus questions (fetchBonusQuestions, placeBonusBets, bonusBets)

dataFormats: "INPUT config: env vars KICKTIPP_EMAIL/KICKTIPP_PASSWORD (highest precedence), else ~/.config/kicktipp-agent/config.ini (ini format, chmod 600) with [auth] email/password(AES-GCM enc.), [community] name, [player] name. Playwright session at ~/.config/kicktipp-agent/session.json. Bet input grammar: match = 'Home vs Away=H:G' (split on LAST '=', then on ' vs '); bonus = 'Question text=Answer' (split on LAST '='). OUTPUT: CLI commands print human-formatted ASCII tables / plain text to stdout, errors to stderr, exit 0/1. MCP tools return JSON.stringify(data,null,2) as a single text content block (NOT structured/typed MCP content) for every tool. Data shapes (TS interfaces in core.ts): TodayMatch{time,home,away,bet,odds{home,draw,away},needsBet}, BetMatch, ScheduleMatch{date,home,away,result}, RankingEntry{position,name,matchdayPoints,bonus,total,isCurrentPlayer}, OverviewPlayer{position,name,matchdays:Record<number,string>,bonus,wins,total,isCurrentPlayer}, TableTeam{position,team,played,points,goalsFor,goalsAgainst,goalDifference,wins,draws,losses}, BonusQuestion{question,selects[{name,options[{value,text}],selected}]}, RulesSection{type:heading|paragraph|table,...}. Odds returned as raw strings, never numeric."
oddsLogic: n/a — the tool SCRAPES and DISPLAYS the kicktipp 'Wettquote' odds (span.quote-heim/quote-remis/quote-gast > span.quote-text) and passes them through to output, but it NEVER predicts or computes a scoreline from odds. There is zero auto-prediction / betting strategy: all bets are supplied by the human or the LLM agent. (The Python ancestor schwalle/kicktipp-betbot reportedly had odds-based auto-betting; this rewrite dropped it.) This is a major gap we can beat.
strengths: Unified core.ts shared by both CLI and MCP server — single source of truth for scraping/betting logic, clean separation
Comprehensive read coverage: today, bets, schedule, leaderboard, overview (5 view types), league table (home/away), rules, communities, players, bonus questions
MCP write tools (place_bets, place_bonus_bets) support dry_run for safe preview, and are clearly labeled DESTRUCTIVE in descriptions — good agent-safety hygiene
MCP instructions string + get_status tool steer the agent through setup (check creds → get_communities → set_community) — thoughtful agent UX
Session persistence via Playwright storageState + session-validity probe = fast repeat runs, automatic re-login on expiry
MCP server reuses one persistent browser across tool calls (avoids per-call ~2-5s cold start)
Credentials never exposed to the LLM (env block / config file), password masked on interactive entry, config + session chmod 600

Robust bet-arg parsing (splits on LAST '=' so team names/questions with '=' mostly survive; ' vs ' separator), duplicate-fixture detection, case-insensitive fixture matching, multi-select bonus support
Handles both US (M/D/YY h:mm AM/PM) and DE (DD.MM.YY HH:MM) date formats — locale resilience
Dismisses Sourcepoint cookie-consent CMP automatically
Has unit tests (vitest) for parse-bet-arg and url builders
weaknesses: NO odds-based or any auto-prediction strategy — cannot suggest/compute scorelines; purely a manual data-entry conduit. Biggest functional gap.

Heavy/ slow: every single operation spins up headless Chromium and does a full page navigation (~2-5s each, admitted in guide.ts). No direct HTTP client. The MCP persistent-browser mitigates repeat calls but cold start + memory footprint are large vs a fetch-based client.

Selectors are brittle and undocumented in provenance: relies on generic `tbody` (first/only) under #kicktipp-content for predict pages, fixed column INDICES ($(cols[1]) home, cols[2] away, cols[3] bet, cols[4] odds; table needs >=10 cols) — any layout/column change silently yields empty results (returns {matches:[]} with no error).
Consent handling only matches English 'Accept and continue' button text — German users (the actual audience for kicktipp.de-style CMP) may not get dismissed, blocking scrapes.

Password 'encryption' is security theater: AES key derived deterministically from hostname+username, so config.ini password is trivially decryptable by anyone with the file. Env-var path warns it's visible via `ps e`.
Hardcoded Bundesliga assumption: matchday clamped 1-34 everywhere (RangeError). Breaks for leagues/competitions with different fixture counts (Champions League, World Cup, 2. Liga playoffs, etc.).
MCP returns everything as a JSON string blob in a single text content — no MCP structured content / output schema / resources / prompts. Token-heavy, not machine-typed.
No pagination/season selector beyond matchday; cannot pick a different season or competition; community is the only scope.
fetchTable uses content.find('table').first() — grabs the FIRST table on the page, very fragile if page adds any table above standings.

placeBonusBets matching logic in core.ts is convoluted/buggy-looking (tries to match question by answers[0] text first, then a fallback loop over raw args) vs the cleaner CLI version — risk of mis-assigning answers.
No rate limiting / retry / backoff / error typing — network or selector failures surface as generic Errors or empty arrays; login failure in CLI does process.exit(1) (bad for a library).
No tests for any scraping/parsing of real HTML (only url + parse-bet-arg unit tests); no fixtures.
CLI has no dry-run for bet (only MCP does); CLI interactive bet requires stdin (unusable in automation).
Single-commit repo, no CI, no versioned releases, likely unmaintained.
No structured logging, no JSON output mode for CLI (only ASCII tables) — hard to script CLI output.
Team/fixture matching is EXACT (case-insensitive) only — no fuzzy matching, so agent must get kicktipp's exact German team names ('FC Bayern München') verbatim or it throws.

approachDetail: Single-file Python script (tipper.py, 132 lines) that drives KickTipp purely over HTTP using the RoboBrowser library (a Mechanize-style wrapper around requests + Werkzeug forms) with BeautifulSoup (parser="html.parser") for HTML parsing. RoboBrowser auto-discovers and submits HTML forms (browser.get_form() / browser.submit_form(form)) and transparently carries the session cookie jar across requests via its internal requests.Session. No JavaScript/browser engine. Math is the only other dependency. Flow in __main__: instantiate RoboBrowser -> login() (interactive prompts, retry loop) -> set_bet_urls(grab_beturl()) to derive the group's betting URLs from a discovered link -> grab_odds() -> calc_results() -> pass_results() submits, prints "Done!".
language: Python 2/3 (uses input(), so Python 3-oriented). Libraries: robobrowser (HTTP form automation), beautifulsoup4 (bs4, html.parser), math (stdlib). README also lists "Windows" as a requirement. No requirements.txt, setup.py, or pinned versions present.
maintenance: Abandoned. Exactly 1 commit total. Last (and only) commit date 2016-01-31 15:09:58 +0100. MIT License, Copyright (c) 2016 Alexander Krooß. Repo origin https://github.com/akrooss/KickTippTipper, single 'master' branch, no tags. Untouched for ~10 years; targets pre-2016 KickTipp HTML and http:// (non-TLS) endpoints that are almost certainly stale today.

auth.csrf: none — no CSRF token is read or sent. The code calls browser.get_form() on the login page and only sets kennung/passwort; any hidden fields RoboBrowser auto-discovers are submitted untouched, but no token is explicitly handled.
auth.sessionReuse: None persisted. Session lives only in the in-memory RoboBrowser instance (its internal requests.Session cookie jar) for the duration of one run. No cookie file, no token caching, no env/credential storage. Every run re-prompts for username/password via input() and re-logs-in. Credentials are read interactively, never stored.
auth.notes: Login uses HTTP (not HTTPS): http://www.kicktipp.de/info/profil/login. Login is done by opening the login page, grabbing the first form via browser.get_form() (no name/index specified), setting form['kennung']=username and form['passwort']=password, then browser.submit_form(form). Success is verified heuristically by did_login_work(): it re-scans the current page for <input type="text" name="kennung">; if that login field is still present, login is deemed failed and the loop re-prompts ('Your email or password was incorrect. Please try again.'). There is no 'stay logged in'/remember-me handling and the well-known KickTipp 'login' cookie is never referenced by name.

dataFormats: Input: interactive stdin prompts only — input(\"Username: \") and input(\"Password: \"). No env vars, no config file, no CLI args/flags. The five possible scorelines are hardcoded Python lists ([1,1],[2,1],[1,2],[3,1],[1,3]) editable inline. Output: none structured — the only output is the literal string \"Done!\" printed to stdout on success, plus error messages during the login retry loop. No JSON/CSV, no logging, no dry-run/preview of computed bets before submission.
oddsLogic: Yes — predicts scores from odds. grab_odds() collects every td.kicktipp-wettquote cell's text as a float in document order, then chunks them into groups of 3 per match: [home_odds(i[0]), draw_odds(i[1]), away_odds(i[2])]. calc_results() per match: diff = math.fabs(i[0]-i[2]) (absolute difference of home-win odds and away-win odds). Rules: if diff < 1.0 -> deuce [1,1] (draw). elif diff > 8.0 (blowout) -> if i[0] > i[1] append team2_greatwin [1,3] else team1_greatwin [3,1]. else (1.0<=diff<=8.0) -> if i[0] > i[1] append team2_win [1,2] else team1_win [2,1]. NOTE the direction test is inconsistent/buggy: it compares home-win odds i[0] against DRAW odds i[1] (not against away odds i[2]) to decide which side wins, and it assigns victory to the higher-odds (i.e. underdog) side — when i[0]>i[1] it gives the AWAY team the win. With decimal odds (lower = favorite) this logic does not reliably back the favorite. Magnitude thresholds (1.0 and 8.0) are also raw odds-difference constants with no normalization, so they behave very differently for low-odds vs high-odds matches.
strengths: Extremely small and readable — entire tool is one 132-line file, trivial to audit
HTTP-direct (no browser engine) makes it fast and dependency-light compared to Selenium/Playwright
Confirms the durable KickTipp HTTP auth contract: POST kennung + passwort to /info/profil/login
Confirms the /<group-slug>/tippabgabe betting-page URL pattern and the m.kicktipp.de mobile mirror
Confirms td.kicktipp-wettquote as the odds container class and that odds appear in home/draw/away triples
Auto-discovers the group slug instead of hardcoding it
Gracefully trims results when some matches on the matchday are already played
weaknesses: Uses http:// (cleartext) for login and all requests — credentials sent without TLS as written
No session/cookie persistence: re-prompts and re-logs-in every run; not automatable/headless (input() blocks)
No env vars, config file, or CLI flags — cannot be scripted or scheduled without modification
No dry-run/preview; it submits bets immediately with zero confirmation
Buggy/inconsistent odds-to-score logic: compares home-odds to DRAW odds and backs the underdog; raw un-normalized thresholds
grab_beturl() is a fragile heuristic (href==link text) that will grab the wrong/first matching link and only ever returns ONE group — no multi-group support
Relies on positional assumptions: chunk odds in 3s, chunk input fields in 2s, get_form() = first form — any layout change breaks it silently
did_login_work() detects failure only by presence of the kennung input; CAPTCHA, rate-limit, or 2FA pages would be misread
No error handling, retries, timeouts, or logging anywhere except the login loop
Abandoned since 2016; targets long-stale HTML and likely-dead http:// endpoints
No tests, no packaging, no requirements pinning
Hardcoded German field names and brittle to bonus/deadline edge cases (no check whether deadline passed)
lessons: COPY the auth contract exactly: POST form fields kennung (user) + passwort (pass) to /info/profil/login — this is the canonical KickTipp login and is corroborated here. But use HTTPS (https://www.kicktipp.de/...), not http://.

COPY the URL templates: https://www.kicktipp.de/<group>/tippabgabe for the bet form. Treat the m.kicktipp.de mobile variant as a legacy fallback only — do not depend on it; modern KickTipp serves a responsive desktop form. Confirm against live HTML before shipping.

COPY the odds selector concept (td.kicktipp-wettquote) as a starting selector, but DO verify current DOM — class names may have changed in 10 years. Capture odds as home/draw/away triples but bind by column semantics, not blind position.

DO BETTER on session handling: persist the KickTipp 'login' cookie (and JSESSIONID) to disk/keychain and reuse it; only re-auth when the session is invalid. This is the single biggest gap for an MCP/CLI that must run unattended.

DO BETTER on login-success detection: don't infer success from absence of the kennung input. Check for an authenticated marker (logout link / profile element / redirect away from /login) and explicitly detect CAPTCHA, rate-limit, and 2FA pages.
DO BETTER on credentials: take them via env vars / config / OS keychain (never interactive-only), so the tool is schedulable and headless. Never hardcode, never send over http://.

DO BETTER on the bet-submission: parse each match row to map score input fields to a specific game by stable ids/names rather than chunking inputs in pairs by position. KickTipp's real field names follow patterns like spieltippForms[...].heimTipp / gastTipp (the _heimTipp/_gastTipp convention) — discover and bind them explicitly, with a dry-run/preview and explicit confirm before POST.
DO BETTER on group handling: support multiple groups/communities explicitly (the slug is part of the URL) instead of a fragile single-link href==text heuristic.

DO BETTER on the prediction model: do not back the underdog. Use proper implied-probability conversion from decimal odds (1/odds, then normalize to remove the bookmaker margin) and pick a scoreline from a calibrated mapping; make thresholds tunable and document them. Keep result templates configurable like this tool does, but choose the favorite correctly.
DO BETTER on robustness: add timeouts, retries, structured logging, deadline checks (don't submit after kickoff), and JSON output so the CLI/MCP layer can consume results programmatically.
ADOPT the partial-matchday handling idea (skip already-played games) but implement it by detecting locked/disabled inputs per row, not by length-diff trimming.
isMcpOrCli: CLI script. Not an MCP server and not a packaged CLI — it is a bare Python script (tipper.py) run directly (python tipper.py) with an `if __name__ == '__main__'` block. No argparse/click, no subcommands, no MCP tools/resources. The only 'commands' are the hardcoded sequence in __main__: login() -> set_bet_urls(grab_beturl()) -> grab_odds() -> calc_results() -> pass_results(). User interaction is limited to two stdin prompts (Username, Password). Functions: login, did_login_work, grab_odds, calc_results, get_keys, pass_results, grab_beturl, set_bet_urls.

approachDetail: Single-file Python script (lambda_function.py, ~346 lines) using Playwright's sync API (playwright.sync_api.sync_playwright) driving headless Chromium. Despite the "serverless" name and AWS-Lambda-shaped entrypoint (lambda_handler(_event,_context) returning {statusCode,body}), it is NOT actually deployed to Lambda anywhere in this repo — there's no SAM/Serverless/CDK/zip packaging. It's packaged as a Docker image (FROM mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/python:v1.44.0-jammy) that runs `python main.py --headless` once and exits. The README claims it's "intended to be run as a job not a service" requiring an EXTERNAL scheduler (cron / GCP Cloud Run code triggers / AWS EventBridge). The repo's own GitHub Actions cron ('33 15 * * *') only REBUILDS the Docker image daily — it does not run the bot. The k8s Deployment runs it as a restart:Always pod (continuously restarting), which contradicts the "job" framing. The TypeScript Playwright files (playwright.config.ts, e2e/, tests/, tests-examples/, package.json) are unused leftover scaffolding from `npm init playwright`; the bot is 100% Python. Note: the lambda's final print says "Sleeping for 30 min" but there is no actual sleep — the process just exits.
language: Python 3 (Playwright sync API, playwright==1.44.0; requests>=2.25.1). Base image mcr.microsoft.com/playwright/python:v1.44.0-jammy. Leftover/unused: TypeScript @playwright/test ^1.44.1, @types/node ^20.14.6.
maintenance: Effectively abandoned / single-snapshot. Exactly 1 commit total; first and last commit both 2024-07-08 15:18:38 +0200. No history, no tags visible, no iteration. The repo is a near-fork of antonengelhardt/kicktipp-bot (credited in README and referenced in k8s/deployment.yaml + docker-compose.yml which still pull ghcr.io/antonengelhardt/kicktipp-bot:amd64, not the author's own image). GitHub Actions docker-publish cron rebuilds the image daily but no code changes since the single 2024 commit.

auth.csrf: none — no CSRF token handling whatsoever. Login is done purely by filling #kennung and #passwort and clicking the 'Anmelden' button via Playwright; no hidden token is read or submitted by the code.
auth.sessionReuse: None. No persistence at all. Every invocation launches a fresh headless Chromium (browser=p.chromium.launch(headless=True); page=browser.new_page()) with a clean context, logs in from scratch, does its work, then browser.close(). No storage_state / cookies dumped or reloaded, no JSESSIONID handling in code — the session lives only inside the in-memory browser context for the duration of one run.
auth.notes: Auth is implicit/browser-managed: Playwright's Chromium holds whatever session cookie KickTipp sets (the real KickTipp login cookie is named 'login', plus JSESSIONID, but the code never names or touches them — it relies entirely on the browser keeping them). Login success is verified ONLY by checking `page.url == BASE_URL` ('https://www.kicktipp.de/') after clicking Anmelden; on failure it just prints 'Login failed!' and CONTINUES executing anyway (no exception/abort). Credentials come from env vars KICKTIPP_EMAIL and KICKTIPP_PASSWORD; lambda_handler raises ValueError if either is None. There is also a cookie-consent banner handled by clicking a button named 'ZUSTIMMEN' (done mid-flow on the tabellen/tippspielplan page, not before login).

endpoints.purpose: Login page; fill #kennung/#passwort and click Anmelden
endpoints.method: GET (page.goto) then form POST via button click

endpoints.purpose: Standings/tables page, scraped to compute average goals of the previous round (sums .col2 game counts and .col4 'x:y' goals across .drei_punkte_regel tables)
Match schedule / results page, scraped over spieltagIndex 8,9,10 to count goals for fulltime vs after-extra-time (n.V.) vs after-penalties games via .kicktipp-abpfiff
Tip submission page; reads each game row (#tippabgabeSpiele), computes a score from bookmaker odds, fills the two score inputs and clicks 'Tipps speichern'
endpoints.method: GET (page.goto, wait_until='commit') then form POST via button click

selectors: button name="ZUSTIMMEN" (cookie-consent accept button, via get_by_role)
button name="Tipps speichern" (save-tips submit, via get_by_role)
tbody tr -> .kicktipp-tippabgabe (per-game tip-entry cell containing the score inputs)
tippabgabe xpath=//input[2] (HOME score input — note: 2nd input, after a hidden/first input)
.col2 (away team name on tippabgabe rows; ALSO reused as game-count column on tabellen tables)
.wettquote-link (bookmaker odds element; inner_text like 'Quote: 1.50 / 3.40 / 5.00' or pipe-separated '1.50 | 3.40 | 5.00')
.kicktipp-abpfiff (final-whistle result element on /tippspielplan; child //span elements: [0]=home goals,[2]=away goals, optional [3]='n.V.' marker)
features: Auto-login to KickTipp and accept cookie consent
Auto-tip ALL open games in one or more competitions based on bookmaker odds
Time-window gating: only tips games starting within KICKTIPP_HOURS_UNTIL_GAME (default 2h) via .kicktipp-time parsing
Computes per-context average goals (regular fulltime, after extra time 'n.V.', after penalties) by scraping prior standings (/tabellen) and prior matchdays (/tippspielplan spieltagIndex 8-10)
Supports up to 4 competition env vars distinguishing scoring rule context: NE (after penalties / need-winner), NV (after extra time), 90M (after 90 min), and a base required competition
'need_winner' logic that breaks predicted draws into a decisive scoreline (with a coin-flip via random.randint when win/loss ratio is exactly 0.5)
Zapier webhook notification per tipped game (POST form data with date/teams/odds/tip)
ntfy push notification per tipped game (POST with HTTP basic auth + X-Title header)
Configurable LOG_LEVEL (DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL)
Dockerized run-once execution; k8s Deployment, docker-compose, and Makefile targets for build/run/push
dataFormats: Input: environment variables only (no config file, no CLI args parsed — '--headless'/'--local' flags are passed to the process but NOT read by argparse; headless is hardcoded True). Required: KICKTIPP_EMAIL, KICKTIPP_PASSWORD, KICKTIPP_NAME_OF_COMPETITION, LOG_LEVEL. Optional: KICKTIPP_NAME_OF_90M_COMPETITION, KICKTIPP_NAME_OF_NV_COMPETITION, KICKTIPP_NAME_OF_NE_COMPETITION, KICKTIPP_HOURS_UNTIL_GAME (default 2), ZAPIER_URL, NTFY_URL, NTFY_USERNAME, NTFY_PASSWORD, LOG_LEVEL, IMAGE_NAME/CONTAINER_NAME (build only). Loaded via .env (Makefile `include .env`/docker --env-file) or k8s ConfigMap+Secret. Output: human-readable stdout prints (team names, odds, computed tip) + Python logging; side effects are the tips written into the KickTipp form and optional Zapier/ntfy POSTs. lambda_handler returns JSON {'statusCode':200,'body':'\"Hello from Lambda!\"'}. No structured machine-readable output (no JSON/CSV result of predictions is emitted).
strengths: Tiny, dependency-light surface (only requests + playwright) — easy to read end to end in one file
Uses Playwright sync API cleanly with role-based locators (get_by_role with German button labels) which are more robust than brittle XPaths for buttons
Run-once job model is the right shape for scheduled prediction (fresh browser per run, then exit) and is genuinely stateless
Sensible odds-to-score heuristic with explicit draw-breaking ('need_winner') logic and context-aware average-goals estimation (fulltime vs extra-time vs penalties)
Multiple deployment artifacts provided (Dockerfile, docker-compose, k8s manifests, Makefile, GitHub Actions image build + cosign signing)
Pluggable notifications (Zapier + ntfy) decoupled behind optional env vars with graceful no-op when unset
weaknesses: HARDCODED tippsaisonId=2801716 in the /tippspielplan URL — season-specific, will silently break/scrape wrong data for any other competition or season; also spieltagIndex hardcoded to range(8,11)

No real session persistence: re-logs in via full browser automation every run; no storage_state reuse, no HTTP-level session, so it's slow and heavy (full Chromium) for what is fundamentally a few form POSTs

Misleading 'serverless'/Lambda framing: lambda_handler exists but there is zero Lambda packaging; actually runs as a long-lived restart:Always k8s pod or a docker run; final 'Sleeping for 30 min' message is a lie (no sleep, process just exits)
Login failure is not fatal — prints 'Login failed!' and keeps going, leading to confusing downstream failures
No CSRF/token handling and login success detected only by exact url equality (page.url == BASE_URL) — fragile to redirects/locale/trailing differences

Massive duplicated block inside enter_tips: the time-parse, time-gate, team-name extraction and game print are copy-pasted twice per game (lines ~170-192 and ~218-240); time_until_game can be referenced before assignment when time_str is '' on the first row
Brittle odds parsing: string-replaces 'Quote: ', then guesses delimiter between ' / ' and ' | '; no handling for missing/locale-formatted odds beyond skipping

Score inputs targeted by positional XPath //input[2] and //input[3] inside .kicktipp-tippabgabe rather than KickTipp's real field names (the site uses inputs named like spieltippForms[...].heimTipp/gastTipp) — order-dependent and fragile

Clicks 'Tipps speichern' once PER GAME inside the loop (a full page POST + navigation per match) instead of filling all rows and saving once — slow and racy; after submit the locators/page state are stale
No tests of the actual bot (the only test files are the default Playwright demo specs); no error handling/retries around navigation or network
Reuses .col2 selector for two different meanings (away-team on tippabgabe vs game-count on tabellen) — easy to misread/break
No timezone-safe datetime handling (uses naive datetime.now() and Europe/Berlin only via Docker TZ); Dockerfile has a typo 'ENV DISPLAY=:99RUN' that mangles the env value
Credentials/secrets passed as plain env; k8s secrets.yaml ships placeholder base64 'data' (should be stringData) and pulls a third party's image (antonengelhardt) not the repo's own
lessons: COPY: the run-once stateless job model and the odds-driven scoring core (win/loss-ratio * expected-goals with explicit draw-breaking for knockout 'need_winner' contexts, plus context-specific average-goals: 90min vs n.V. vs penalties). This is a reasonable baseline prediction strategy we can offer as one CLI/MCP strategy.

COPY (as verified constants): the exact KickTipp auth + form contract — login at /info/profil/login/ with fields #kennung (email) and #passwort (password), submit button label 'Anmelden', cookie banner button 'ZUSTIMMEN', and the tip page at /{competition}/tippabgabe with table #tippabgabeSpiele, per-game cells .kicktipp-tippabgabe, kickoff time .kicktipp-time (format '%d.%m.%y %H:%M'), team cells .col1/.col2, odds in .wettquote-link, and save button 'Tipps speichern'. Reuse these strings but validate them live.

DO BETTER — ditch the browser for the hot path: KickTipp's tippabgabe is plain server-rendered HTML forms. We should go http-direct (requests/httpx + BeautifulSoup/lxml) for login + scrape + submit, and persist the 'login' session cookie (and JSESSIONID) to disk so we don't relaunch Chromium every run. Keep Playwright only as an optional fallback for consent walls / JS edge cases.

DO BETTER — target real form field names, not positional //input[2]/[3]: parse the actual input name attributes (KickTipp uses indexed names like spieltippForms[N].heimTipp / .gastTipp plus hidden game-id fields and any CSRF/_token hidden inputs) and submit them in one POST per matchday, not one click per game. This is faster, atomic, and far less fragile.

DO BETTER — never hardcode tippsaisonId / spieltagIndex. Discover the current season id and matchday dynamically by scraping the schedule nav, and let the user select matchday/season. The 2801716 hardcode is exactly the kind of bug that makes a clone rot.
DO BETTER — fail loudly on login: verify login by presence of an authenticated element or absence of the login form, not page.url string equality, and abort with a clear error instead of continuing.
DO BETTER — handle CSRF properly: read and resubmit hidden token/form fields from the fetched tippabgabe page (the browser hid this complexity; an http-direct client must do it explicitly).

DO BETTER — emit structured output: our MCP tools and CLI should return machine-readable JSON (per-game predicted score, odds, kickoff, whether-submitted) so the prediction step is separable from the submit step (dry-run/preview mode). This repo prints to stdout only.

DO BETTER — separate concerns into discrete MCP tools/CLI subcommands (login/list-matches/predict/submit/standings) instead of one monolithic tip_all_games() that scrapes, predicts, fills and notifies in a single tangled loop with duplicated blocks.
COPY the optional notification idea (ntfy/Zapier) but make notifications a generic post-submit hook; AVOID copying the per-game webhook spam inside the fill loop.

AVOID their 'serverless' misnomer: if we want true serverless, actually package for Lambda/Cloud Run with a thin handler and an external scheduler (EventBridge/Cloud Scheduler), and don't ship a restart:Always pod claiming to be a job.
AVOID their delimiter-guessing odds parser and time-before-assignment bug; normalize odds robustly and gate on kickoff time safely.
oddsLogic: YES — predicts scores from bookmaker odds. Function predict_with_win_loss_ratio(win, loss, expected_goals, need_winner) where win=odds[0] (home win odds) and loss=odds[2] (away win odds), passed as floats. NOTE the variables are mislabeled: it names the raw odds 'win'/'loss' but uses them as weights. Steps: (1) loss_ratio = win / (win + loss)  [i.e. home_odds / (home_odds + away_odds)]; a HIGHER home win-odds (less likely home win) yields a higher loss_ratio. (2) loss_goals_estimate = expected_goals * loss_ratio; win_goals_estimate = expected_goals - loss_goals_estimate. (3) win_bet = round(win_goals_estimate); loss_bet = round(loss_goals_estimate). win_bet maps to the HOME score (tip[0]), loss_bet to the AWAY score (tip[1]). 'expected_goals' is the context-specific average total goals computed by scraping: avg_goals_preround from /tabellen (.col4 totals / .col2 game counts, with x2 doubling because each game appears for both teams), then avg_goals_fulltime = (goals_preround/2 + fulltime_goals)/(games_preround/2 + games_fulltime); avg_goals_nv (after extra time) and avg_goals_ne (after penalties) from /tippspielplan .kicktipp-abpfiff spans over spieltagIndex 8-10. Defaults when no data: preround avg 2.25, nv 3, ne 3.8333. need_winner (true only for the NE/penalties or fallback knockout context) breaks a predicted draw: if loss_ratio<0.5 bump the home goal up (or away down) to keep total near expected_goals; if exactly 0.5 a coin flip (random.randint(0,1)) adds a goal to one side; if >0.5 favor the away side. Returns (win_bet, loss_bet, round(1-loss_ratio,2)=home win probability proxy, round(expected_goals,2), round(win_goals_estimate,2), round(loss_goals_estimate,2)). The draw odds (odds[1]) are captured and sent to notifications but NOT used in the score calculation.
isMcpOrCli: Neither MCP nor a real CLI. It is a single-shot Python script with one entrypoint lambda_function.py:lambda_handler(_event,_context) (AWS-Lambda-signature shaped, returns {'statusCode':200,'body':json.dumps('Hello from Lambda!')}), also runnable via `if __name__=='__main__': lambda_handler(None,None)`. It accepts NO real CLI arguments — Makefile/Dockerfile pass '--headless' and '--local' but nothing parses them (no argparse/sys.argv usage); headless is hardcoded True. All configuration is via environment variables. Architecture (one flat module): lambda_handler -> validates LOG_LEVEL + required env -> tip_all_games() which (a) launches headless Chromium via sync_playwright, (b) login(page), (c) scrapes /tabellen and /tippspielplan to compute average goals, (d) accepts cookie banner, (e) tip_all_games_for_competition() dispatches to enter_tips() once per configured competition (NE, NV, 90M, or fallback base), (f) enter_tips() loops game rows, computes score via predict_with_win_loss_ratio(), fills inputs, clicks 'Tipps speichern' per game, and fires send_zapier_webhook()/send_ntfy_notification(). Deployment is container-based: Docker image (playwright/python base) run once, or as a k8s Deployment (restart:Always), docker-compose, or any external scheduler (cron/EventBridge/Cloud Run triggers) per README. GitHub Actions (docker-publish.yml) builds+cosign-signs the image daily on cron '33 15 * * *' but does not execute the bot.
